# Genesis

> The Claude that builds Claudes. Bootstrap fully-equipped Claude Code projects in under two minutes.

Genesis is an open-source Claude Code project bootstrapper created by David Summers. It scaffolds fully-equipped Claude Code development environments from a brief project description.

## What Genesis Does

Genesis takes a natural language project description and generates a complete Claude Code workspace including:

- CLAUDE.md with stack-specific rules, coding standards, and testing mandates
- Specialised agents (test-runner, code-reviewer, doc-writer, plus domain-specific)
- Skills (/test, /lint, /review, /commit, plus domain-specific)
- Settings with permissions, formatter hooks, and stop hooks
- MCP server configurations for integrations (databases, GitHub, Playwright, AWS)
- Memory files with user profile and project context
- Application boilerplate (entry points, package config, test setup)
- Git repository with initial commit

## How It Works

Genesis follows a four-phase workflow:

1. Interview: extracts project name, purpose, tech stack, and integrations from the user's description. Asks 1-4 follow-up questions only for missing details.
2. Plan: consults stack profiles and agent catalogues to assemble a generation plan. Presents agents, skills, MCP servers, and folder layout for user approval.
3. Generate: creates the entire project directory in a single pass using templates and stack-specific references.
4. Finalise: initialises git, updates the project registry, prints a summary with next steps.

## Supported Stacks

- Node.js / TypeScript (ESLint, Prettier, vitest/jest, ESM)
- Python (ruff, pytest, mypy/pyright, PEP 621)
- Go (gofmt, golangci-lint, table-driven tests)
- Rust (rustfmt, clippy, cargo test, thiserror/anyhow)
- Ruby (RuboCop, RSpec/Minitest, Bundler)
- Java / Kotlin (Gradle/Maven, Spotless, JUnit 5)

## Architecture

Genesis uses five specialised agents:

- Interviewer: reads user input, extracts project details
- Planner: selects agents, skills, MCP servers, folder layout
- Scaffold Writer: generates application boilerplate and config
- Claude Infra Writer: creates CLAUDE.md, agents, skills, memory files
- Finaliser: git init, registry update, summary output

Reference material includes 7 file templates, 6 stack profiles, and an agent catalogue with 30+ archetypes.

## User Configuration

Genesis separates updatable code from user-specific configuration:

- personalisation.md: locale, output style, role, experience level, project defaults
- environment.md: platform, shell, paths, package manager

These files are gitignored and preserved across Genesis updates.

## Skills

- /genesis: bootstrap a new project (main entry point)
- /registry: view all projects created by Genesis
- /validate: check a generated project is complete and well-formed
- /update: pull the latest Genesis updates
